description: Fit a deterministic SIR (Susceptible-Infected-Recovered) model to the 1978 influenza outbreak in a British boarding school (population N=763) using the 'influenza_1978_school' dataset from the 'outbreaks' R package. Estimate the transmission rate (beta) and recovery rate (gamma) by minimizing the Sum of Squared Errors (SSE) between the model's predicted infection prevalence (using the 'in_bed' column) and the observed data. Calculate the Basic Reproduction Number (R0 = beta / gamma). Save the results including beta, gamma, R0, and SSE to /home/ga/RProjects/output/sir_parameters.csv and generate a plot comparing the observed data points to the fitted model trajectory, saved to /home/ga/RProjects/output/sir_fit_plot.png. Write the analysis script in /home/ga/RProjects/sir_analysis.R.
